parameters.name: file
parameters.label: File
parameters.description: The path and name of the point file to load. Point file formats are those found in .  You can specify files on the internet using ...

parameters.name: reload
parameters.label: Reload
parameters.description: Change from 0 to 1 to force the file to reload. Useful when the file changes or did not exist at first.

parameters.name: reloadpulse
parameters.label: Reload Pulse
parameters.description: Immediately reload the point data file.

parameters.name: red
parameters.label: Red
parameters.description: Select one of the available point data channels to place it into the red channel of the output image. Selecting One or Zero will place the constance value into the output channel.

parameters.name: green
parameters.label: Green
parameters.description: Select one of the available point data channels to place it into the green channel of the output image. Selecting One or Zero will place the constance value into the output channel.

parameters.name: blue
parameters.label: Blue
parameters.description: Select one of the available point data channels to place it into the blue channel of the output image. Selecting One or Zero will place the constance value into the output channel.

parameters.name: alpha
parameters.label: Alpha
parameters.description: Select one of the available point data channels to place it into the alpha channel of the output image. Selecting One or Zero will place the constance value into the output channel.

parameters.name: playmode
parameters.label: Play Mode
parameters.description: Specifies the method used to play the animation, there are 3 options.

parameters.name: play
parameters.label: Play
parameters.description: Animation plays when 1, stops when 0.

parameters.name: speed
parameters.label: Speed
parameters.description: This is a speed multiplier which only works when Play Mode is

parameters.name: cue
parameters.label: Cue
parameters.description: Jumps to Cue Point when set to 1. Only available when Play Mode is Sequential.

parameters.name: cuepulse
parameters.label: Cue Pulse
parameters.description: Instantly jumps to the Cue Point position in the movie.

parameters.name: cuepoint
parameters.label: Cue Point
parameters.description: Set any index in the animation as a point to jump to.

parameters.name: cuepointunit
parameters.label: Cue Point Unit
parameters.description: Select the units for this parameter from Index, Frames, Seconds, and Fraction (percentage).

parameters.name: cuebehavior
parameters.label: Cue Behavior
parameters.description: Customize the Cue parameter's behavior.

parameters.name: index
parameters.label: Index
parameters.description: This parameter explicitly sets the file sequence position when Play Mode is set to Specify Index. The units menu on the right lets you specify the index in the following units: Index, Frames, Seconds,

parameters.name: indexunit
parameters.label: Index Unit
parameters.description: Select the units for this parameter from Index, Frames, Seconds, and Fraction (percentage).

parameters.name: loopcrossfade
parameters.label: Loop Crossfade
parameters.description: Crossfades the beginning and end of the animation together to create a smooth transition when looping. If the movie uses Trim options, it will crossfade Trim Start with Trim End positions.

parameters.name: loopcrossfadeunit
parameters.label: Loop Crossfade Unit
parameters.description: Select the units for this parameter from Index, Frames, Seconds, and Fraction (percentage).

parameters.name: stepsize
parameters.label: Step Size
parameters.description: Sets how many frames to skip before displaying next frame. For example, a StepSize of 30 will display every 30th frame. The timing of the animation playback does not change, so with a Step Size of 30

parameters.name: audioloop
parameters.label: Audio Loop
parameters.description: This menu helps you determine how to treat the audio as the end of an animation approaches. This is needed because in some cases of playing an animation, like when driving with an index, the will not

parameters.name: imageindexing
parameters.label: Image Sequence Indexing
parameters.description: Determines how a file sequence is ordered.

parameters.name: interp
parameters.label: Interpolate Frames
parameters.description: Interpolates between frames based based on exact time. For example, if the index (in frames) is 1.5, then frames 1 and 2 will be blended 50-50. If the index is 1.7 then 30% of frame 1 is blended with

parameters.name: loadingerrorimage
parameters.label: Loading/Error Image
parameters.description: When the file can not be loaded for some reason, select what to display instead.

parameters.name: trim
parameters.label: Trim
parameters.description: Enables the parameters below to set trim in and out points.

parameters.name: tstart
parameters.label: Trim Start
parameters.description: Trim the starting point of the movie.

parameters.name: tstartunit
parameters.label: Trim Start Unit
parameters.description: Select the units for this parameter from Index, Frames, Seconds, and Fraction (percentage).

parameters.name: tend
parameters.label: Trim End
parameters.description: Trim the ending point of the movie.

parameters.name: tendunit
parameters.label: Trim End Unit
parameters.description: Select the units for this parameter from Index, Frames, Seconds, and Fraction (percentage).

parameters.name: textendleft
parameters.label: Extend Left
parameters.description: Determines how the Point File In handles animation positions that lie before the Trim Start position. For example, if Trim Start is set to 1, and the animation's current index is -10, the Extend Left

parameters.name: textendright
parameters.label: Extend Right
parameters.description: Determines how the Point File In handles animation positions that lie after the Trim End position. For example, if Trim End is set to 20, and the animation's current index is 25, the Extend Right menu

parameters.name: overridesample
parameters.label: Override Sample Rate
parameters.description: Turn On to change the sample rate of the movie. When loading an image sequence, use these parameters to set the playback speed for the sequence.

parameters.name: samplerate
parameters.label: Sample Rate
parameters.description: Set the sample rate for playback when 'Override ' above is On.

parameters.name: prereadframes
parameters.label: Pre-Read Frames
parameters.description: Sets how many animation frames TouchDesigner reads ahead and stores in memory. The Point File In will read and decode frames of the animation into CPU memory before they are used, this can eliminate p

parameters.name: frametimeout
parameters.label: Frame Read Timeout
parameters.description: The time (in milliseconds) TouchDesigner will wait for a frame from the hard drive before giving up. If the Disk Read Timeout time is reached, that frame is simply skipped. This also works for network

parameters.name: frametimeoutstrat
parameters.label: Frame Timeout Strategy
parameters.description: When on, if the Disk Read Timeout is reached TouchDesigner will use the latest available frame in place of the skipped frame.

parameters.name: alwaysloadinitial
parameters.label: Always Load Initial Frame
parameters.description: If this parameter is turned on, then for the first loaded frame the Frame Read Timeout will be ignored, and it will always wait for the first frame to ensure the node always starts up with a valid ima

parameters.name: opentimeout
parameters.label: File Open Timeout
parameters.description: The time (in milliseconds) TouchDesigner will wait for a file to open. If the Disk Open Timout is reached, the Point File In will stop waiting and make its image all black, with a grey square in the b

parameters.name: updateimage
parameters.label: Update Image
parameters.description: Image will not update when set to 0. Animation index will continue to move forward but the output image will not update.

parameters.name: maxdecodecpus
parameters.label: Max Decode CPUs
parameters.description: Limit the maximum number of CPUs that will be used to decode certain file codecs that are capable of multi-CPU decoding.

parameters.name: highperfread
parameters.label: High Performance Read
parameters.description: This option should be used when playing back files that require very high SSD read speeds. It greatly improves read performance in those cases. It should not be used for low resolution or low data rat

parameters.name: highperfreadfactor
parameters.label: High Performance Read Factor
parameters.description: When doing high performance reads, this parameter controls the size of the read operations that are done on disk. Whatever the largest operation the codec asks to be done, this is multiplied by the re

parameters.name: hwdecode
parameters.label: Hardware Decode
parameters.description: Controls if this node should use hardware decoding via the Nvidia hardware decoder chip. You can check if hardware decoding is being used using the Info , 'hardware_decode' channel. This parameter doe

examples.label: Configure Red and Green
examples.description: Common settings from 10 real Derivative examples
